channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the Taurid meteor shower
item.description: With the Taurid meteor shower now hitting the night skies worldwide, look for what could be a celestial treat. You might see shooting stars, and maybe even fireballs, the biggest and brightest meteors. As the full moon begins to wane after Nov.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: San Francisco’s mayor-elect picks Sam Altman to co-chair transition team
item.description: San Francisco Mayor-elect Daniel Lurie has picked OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to help run his transition team as he prepares to take office in January, Lurie said.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: A federal judge in Delaware has dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: Randall Chase, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: El Capitan bumps Frontier to claim world’s fastest supercomputer title
item.description: The new El Capitan system at the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory in California made its debut at No. 1 on the latest TOP500 list of the world’s most powerful supercomputers, released today. The Frontier system at Oak Ridge National Laboratory in Tennessee moved down to the No.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Spin 3.0 supports polyglot development using Wasm components
item.description: Fermyon has released Spin 3.0, a major update to its open source developer tool for building serverless WebAssembly applications. The new release introduces polyglot programming to ease development. The update to Fermyon’s Spin framework was introduced November 11.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: T-Mobile hacked in massive Chinese cyber-espionage: Wall St. Journal
item.description: It was part of a campaign to spy on the phones of high-value intelligence targets, the Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built
item.description: A new challenge looks to digitally recreate the planning of each module of the International Space Station. The post A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: For November, Patch Tuesday includes three Windows zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft’s November Patch Tuesday release addresses 89 vulnerabilities in Windows, SQL Server, .NET and Microsoft Office — and three zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43451, CVE-2024-49019 and CVE-2024-49039) that mean a patch now recommendation for Windows platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The EU seeks proposals for AI that should be banned
item.description: The EU, which is now developing guidelines for how the region’s new AI law must be complied with, has started collecting opinions in two areas via an online survey. The first area involves how the law should define AI systems (compared to traditional software).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: O2's AI Solution Tackles Telephone Scammers: A Game-Changer for Britons?
item.description: O2 introduces Daisy, an AI granny to prevent telephone scams in the UK.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Strategies to navigate the pitfalls of cloud costs
item.description: In my line of work, I often hear CIOs and CFOs say, “Cloud providers are bleeding us dry.” As ever-increasing cloud bills roll in, they’re demanding answers about what they’re paying for and why they’re paying so much. Public cloud providers are eager to oppose this line of questioning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: The Australian government's plan to ban children from social media to protect them from harm has won almost universal political support.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Millions in high speed internet grants awarded in rural New York
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and Gov. Kathy Hochul announced the funds for open-access fiber, wireless hubs.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, NewsNation

item.title: Polymarket at center of political storm after reported FBI raid
item.description: Prediction betting market Polymarket and its founder and CEO Shayne Coplan have found themselves at the center of a debate about political betting after the FBI reportedly raided Coplan’s home on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cisco amps up Splunk observability platform
item.description: As promised, Cisco is making changes to its observability product portfolio to help customers better manage applications across on-premises, hybrid, and multi-cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: F5 gateway works to protect and manage AI applications
item.description: A new gateway from F5 is aimed at helping enterprises to better manage AI security and delivery. The F5 AI Gateway offers an automated way for customers to secure and manage interactions among AI applications, APIs, and large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: At CBP, automated software testing is integral to the mission
item.description: Some parts of the system are more difficult to automate, including operations like biometric fingerprinting. The post At CBP, automated software testing is integral to the mission first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network
